mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.25% represents a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates. A sustained high FEDFUNDS level (above 5.0%) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1772K units signal robust residential construction demand and forward economic activity. Rising HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction-linked corporate earnings expand and consumer wealth effects from housing appreciation support equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1772K units as of September 2006
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with HOUST levels (Spearman rho < 0.10), or housing starts decline more than 15% quarter-over-quarter without corresponding SPX decline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135.8M persons and unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.4% jointly indicate a tight labor market. When UNRATE remains below 4.5% while PAYEMS grows, this signals sustained consumer income and spending power, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days through higher corporate profit expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135844K and UNRATE at 4.4% as of October 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS growth > 0% versus when either condition fails, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.1 reflects current manufacturing and utility output capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO over 3 months signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines > 1.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) at 363.7B dollars measure consumer spending momentum. When RSAFS growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 5.0%, this dual signal indicates demand destruction from monetary tightening, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363673M and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of October 2006
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met versus when either fails, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 202.7 combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates the Fed is maintaining real rates in restrictive territory. If CPIAUCSL growth accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, the Fed will likely maintain or raise rates further, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprice rate expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of October 2006
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant negative correlation with the joint condition (Spearman rho > -0.08), or inflation-rate combinations fail to predict returns better than FEDFUNDS alone (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11432.9B dollars represents the broadest measure of economic activity. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized over two consecutive quarters, this signals late-cycle weakness and recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% for two consecutive quarters, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.4% represents near-full employment. A sharp rise in UNRATE of more than 0.4 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings compression from reduced consumer spending.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.4pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
